input.headline: How Cold War foes became close colleagues — until toilets got in the way

raw.headline: How Cold War foes became close colleagues — until toilets got in the way

winner: Why did the Cold War rivals refuse to high-five? They were afraid they might touch each other's toilet seats.

candidates.text: Why did former Cold War adversaries struggle to become best friends? Because their ideological differences were flushed down the drain... literally!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Puns on 'flushed down the drain' tie ideology to toilets humorously; concise and specific, with a surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the former Cold War enemies have a spat at the United Nations? Because they couldn't agree on whose turn it was to flush the Soviet toilet paper!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild surprise element, but the humor feels forced and relies on an awkward premise (toilet paper in the UN). It lacks natural flow and broader comedic appeal."}

candidates.text: Why did the Cold War rivals refuse to high-five? They were afraid they might touch each other's toilet seats.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and ties the headline's theme to a humorous situation, but the connection between Cold War rivals and toilet seats feels forced and lacks specificity. The humor is mild and relies on a common bathroom joke rather than a clever twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the Soviet and American astronauts have trouble becoming friends during spacewalks? Because they couldn't see eye to eye due to the international differences in toilet standards!
When it comes to diplomacy, there's nothing that can flush relations faster than a disagreement over toilet paper brands.
